sanskrit: मनुष्याणां सहस्रेषु कश्िचद्यतति सिद्धये । यततामपि सिद्धानां कश्िचन्मां वेत्ति तत्त्वतः ॥
hindiTranslation: हजारों मनुष्योंमें कोई एक वास्तविक सिद्धिके लिये यत्न करता है और उन यत्न करनेवाले सिद्धोंमें कोई एक ही मुझे तत्त्वसे जानता है ॥
englishTranslation: Among thousands of people, one may strive for perfection; among those striving, one may know Me in truth.
essence: True knowing is rarer than striving, even among the successful.
speaker: Krishna
simpleMeaning: Many people try to reach perfection, but very few reach true understanding of Krishna. Effort is common; real realization is rare.
simpleInsight: It leaves you humble: sincere effort matters, but deep understanding asks for more than effort alone.
detailedMeaning: On the battlefield, Arjuna has already been told that Krishna can be known fully. Krishna now makes the point sharper: countless people seek achievement, but only a rare few come to know him as he truly is. This prepares Arjuna for a deeper teaching.
modernRelevance: You read a dozen books, follow every step, and still feel something missing. The gap is not more information — it is the rare shift into direct understanding.
translationLiteral: मनुष्याणाम् (of people) / सहस्रेषु (among thousands) / कश्चित् (some one) / यतति (strives) / सिद्धये (for perfection) / यतताम् अपि (even among those striving) / सिद्धानाम् (of the perfected) / कश्चित् (some one) / माम् (Me) / वेत्ति (knows) / तत्त्वतः (in truth)
